exercises.name: Crunches
exercises.description: Classic abdominal move for strengthening the front core and improving trunk control.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift your shoulders by tightening the abs without yanking on the neck.
Lower slowly until your upper back lightly touches the floor, then repeat.
tips: Keep a fist-sized gap between chin and chest.
Press the lower back gently toward the floor.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you crunch up, inhale as you lower down.
commonMistakes: Pulling the head forward with the hands.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Twisting core exercise targeting abs and obliques for a stronger midsection.
steps: Lie on your back with hands behind your head and knees lifted.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Rotate your torso to bring opposite elbow and knee toward each other while extending the other leg.
Return through the center and alternate sides in a smooth rhythm.
tips: Rotate through the ribs, not just the elbows.
Fully extend the straight leg without arching the back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on each twist, inhale through center.
commonMistakes: Moving only the elbows instead of rotating the torso.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Reverse Crunches
exercises.description: Lower-ab focused movement that teaches pelvic control and core tension.
steps: Lie flat with arms by your sides and knees bent to ninety degrees.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Curl the pelvis to bring knees toward the chest instead of swinging the legs.
Lower the hips back down with control before the next rep.
tips: Think about lifting the tailbone, not throwing the feet upward.
Keep the movement small and controlled.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the hips curl up, inhale on the way down.
commonMistakes: Using momentum and kicking the legs.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Leg Raises
exercises.description: Straight-leg core drill that challenges the lower abs and hip control.
steps: Lie on your back with legs straight and hands under your hips if needed.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Raise both legs until they are roughly vertical while keeping the lower back stable.
Lower the legs slowly without letting the heels crash down.
tips: Bend the knees slightly if hamstrings feel too tight.
Only lower as far as you can without the back arching.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as legs rise, inhale as they lower.
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back off the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Flutter Kicks
exercises.description: Small alternating kicks that keep the lower abs under constant tension.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and core braced.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift both legs a few inches and alternate short up-down kicks.
Finish the count, then lower the legs with control.
tips: Keep the kicks quick but small.
Press the lower back into the mat throughout.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale softly every few kicks, inhale steadily through the nose.
commonMistakes: Lifting the legs too high and losing core tension.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Russian Twists
exercises.description: Seated twisting exercise that works the obliques and deep core stabilizers.
steps: Sit with knees bent, chest lifted, and heels lightly on the floor or lifted.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Rotate your torso from side to side, reaching hands beside the hips.
Return to center tall after each twist instead of collapsing.
tips: Keep the spine long instead of rounding the lower back.
Slow down and touch each side with control.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you twist, inhale back through the middle.
commonMistakes: Swinging the arms without turning the torso.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.description: Full-core isometric hold that builds abdominal endurance and posture strength.
steps: Place forearms on the floor with elbows under shoulders and legs extended.
Lift the body into a straight line from head to heels by squeezing abs and glutes.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower one knee at a time and rest before the next set.
tips: Push the floor away to keep the shoulders active.
Keep hips level instead of sagging or hiking.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take short nasal breaths while keeping the belly tight.
commonMistakes: Dropping the hips and stressing the lower back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Side-body hold that strengthens obliques and helps trim waistline support muscles.
steps: Lie on one side with elbow under shoulder and legs stacked.
Lift hips off the floor so your body forms one straight line.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the hips slowly, switch sides, and repeat.
tips: Press firmly through the supporting forearm.
Stack shoulders and hips one above the other.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly into the ribs while keeping the waist lifted.
commonMistakes: Rolling the chest down toward the floor.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Plank Hip Dips
exercises.description: Forearm plank variation that lights up the obliques and deep core.
steps: Start in a forearm plank with feet slightly wider than hip-width.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Rotate the hips to tap one side toward the floor, then sweep to the other side.
Return to a flat plank after each side-to-side dip.
tips: Move from the torso, not just the feet.
Keep shoulders steady over elbows.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the hips dip, inhale back through center.
commonMistakes: Bouncing the hips without control.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Fast plank cardio move that raises heart rate while training the core.
steps: Begin in a high plank with hands under shoulders.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs quickly, and keep alternating.
Slow down briefly if form slips, then finish the rep target.
tips: Keep shoulders stacked over wrists.
Tap the floor softly with each foot change.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe out every two to three switches and keep inhaling through the nose.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips bounce high in the air.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: V-Ups
exercises.description: Advanced abdominal movement that folds the body into a V shape.
steps: Lie long on your back with arms overhead and legs straight.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift arms and legs together to meet near the middle while keeping them as straight as possible.
Lower back down slowly so the core stays loaded.
tips: Use a small knee bend if full straight legs are too hard.
Control the lowering phase to protect the back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you fold up, inhale as you lower.
commonMistakes: Throwing the arms and legs up with momentum.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Low-impact core drill that teaches bracing and lower-back protection.
steps: Lie on your back with arms up and knees bent above hips.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Extend one arm and the opposite leg without letting the lower back lift.
Return to the start and alternate sides slowly.
tips: Move only as far as you can keep the ribs down.
Think of flattening the floor with your lower back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on each reach, inhale when returning to center.
commonMistakes: Arching the back as the leg lowers.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Heel Taps
exercises.description: Gentle side-reaching move that targets the obliques without jumping.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and shoulders slightly lifted.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Reach one hand toward the same-side heel, then alternate sides.
Keep the shoulders hovering until the set is complete.
tips: Use short controlled reaches instead of jerking.
Keep the chin neutral and eyes upward.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on each side tap, inhale through the middle.
commonMistakes: Dropping the shoulders fully between taps.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Toe Touches
exercises.description: Upper-ab reach exercise performed with legs raised toward the ceiling.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended upward and arms reaching to the sky.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Crunch upward to reach toward your toes without swinging the arms.
Lower the shoulders down with control and repeat.
tips: Keep legs as straight as comfortable.
Lift from the ribs rather than from the neck.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you reach up, inhale as you lower.
commonMistakes: Trying to touch the toes by jerking the shoulders.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Scissor Kicks
exercises.description: Alternating straight-leg crossing move that challenges the lower core.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and core pulled in.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift the legs slightly off the floor and cross them over-under in an alternating pattern.
Finish the count, uncross, and lower slowly.
tips: Keep the crossing small and controlled.
Support your hips with your hands if needed.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale softly every few crosses, inhale steadily through the nose.
commonMistakes: Letting the lower back arch off the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Plank Shoulder Taps
exercises.description: Anti-rotation plank move that trains the abs to resist shifting.
steps: Set up in a high plank with feet wider than hips.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand while keeping the hips square, then alternate.
Place the hand down softly each rep and stay balanced.
tips: Keep the legs active to reduce body sway.
Move slower if your hips are rocking.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale during each tap, inhale as the hand returns to the floor.
commonMistakes: Twisting the hips side to side.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Knee-to-Elbow Plank
exercises.description: Dynamic plank exercise that combines crunching and shoulder stability.
steps: Start in a high plank with the body long and strong.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Drive one knee out and toward the same-side elbow, then return and switch sides.
Place the foot back quietly before the next rep.
tips: Round slightly through the upper back to feel the abs engage.
Keep the supporting shoulder directly over the wrist.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as knee meets elbow, inhale as the leg reaches back.
commonMistakes: Stepping the foot forward instead of driving from the core.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Boat Hold (Naukasana)
exercises.description: Seated balancing hold that strengthens the entire core and improves posture.
steps: Sit tall with knees bent and hands lightly behind the thighs.
Lean back slightly, lift the feet, and hold a proud chest in a V position.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the feet down gently and sit upright to recover.
tips: Keep the chest open rather than rounding the spine.
Start with bent knees before trying straighter legs.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Use slow nasal breathing while keeping the belly drawn in.
commonMistakes: Collapsing the chest and rounding the lower back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Plank Jacks
exercises.description: Plank variation that adds light cardio while forcing the core to stay braced.
steps: Begin in a high plank with feet together.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump the feet out and back in while keeping shoulders over wrists.
Land softly and keep the hips level for every rep.
tips: Step the feet out and in if jumping is too much.
Keep the stomach tight to stop the lower back from sagging.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe out on the jump apart, inhale on the jump together.
commonMistakes: Bouncing the hips high with each jump.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.description: Full-body calorie burner that mixes squat, plank, and jump for fat loss.
steps: Stand tall, squat down, and place your hands on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Kick the feet back to plank, return them under the hips, and stand or jump up.
Land softly and flow into the next rep.
tips: Step back instead of jumping if you need less impact.
Keep the chest lifted as you stand up from the squat.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you jump or stand up, inhale as you move down to plank.
commonMistakes: Rounding the back while dropping to the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Surya Namaskar
exercises.description: Traditional sun salutation flow that warms the whole body; one set equals 12 rounds.
steps: Stand tall at the top of the mat with palms joined near the chest.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Flow through the classic sun salutation sequence with steady transitions and matched breath.
Complete all rounds with smooth control, then stand quietly for a few breaths.
tips: Move at a pace that lets you breathe through the full flow.
Keep the knees soft during forward folds.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the lifts and lengthening phases, exhale on folds and lowering phases.
commonMistakes: Racing through the rounds without syncing movement and breath.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Tadasana
exercises.description: Foundational standing pose that improves posture, grounding, and body awareness.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width or together and arms by your sides.
Lengthen through the spine, root the feet down, and lift gently through the crown.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Relax the arms and step out of the pose softly.
tips: Spread the toes and balance weight evenly.
Keep the chin level and ribs stacked over hips.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take slow breaths through the nose while standing tall.
commonMistakes: Locking the knees and leaning back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Utkatasana
exercises.description: Chair pose strengthens legs and core while elevating the heart rate slightly.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width and arms by your sides.
Bend the knees, send hips back, and raise the arms while keeping chest lifted.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Press through the feet to stand tall and release the arms.
tips: Keep more weight in the heels than the toes.
Draw the lower ribs in so the back does not overarch.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lengthen the spine, exhale to sit deeper into the pose.
commonMistakes: Letting the knees collapse inward.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Virabhadrasana I
exercises.description: Warrior I opens the chest and hips while building lower-body endurance.
steps: Step one foot back and turn it out slightly while the front knee bends.
Square the chest forward and reach both arms overhead.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the arms, step forward, and switch sides.
tips: Keep the front knee stacked above the ankle.
Lift through the side waist instead of dumping into the low back.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lengthen upward, exhale to settle into the lunge.
commonMistakes: Forcing the back heel flat at the cost of balance.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Virabhadrasana II
exercises.description: Warrior II strengthens legs and improves balance, stamina, and focus.
steps: Take a wide stance with one foot turned out and the back foot slightly in.
Bend the front knee, extend the arms wide, and gaze over the front hand.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Straighten the leg, lower the arms, and repeat on the other side.
tips: Keep the torso centered between both legs.
Press the outer edge of the back foot into the floor.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to grow tall, exhale to root into the legs.
commonMistakes: Leaning the chest too far over the front thigh.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Trikonasana
exercises.description: Triangle pose lengthens the side body and opens hamstrings and hips.
steps: Stand wide with one foot turned out and arms extended at shoulder height.
Reach forward and tip the torso to place one hand on shin while the other arm lifts.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Rise up with a strong core and switch sides.
tips: Keep both sides of the waist long.
Rest the lower hand lightly instead of dumping weight into it.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lengthen, exhale to rotate the chest open.
commonMistakes: Collapsing the bottom side waist.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Adho Mukha Svanasana
exercises.description: Downward dog stretches the back body and strengthens shoulders and arms.
steps: Start on hands and knees with fingers spread wide.
Lift hips up and back to form an inverted V while pressing the chest toward the thighs.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the knees back to the floor to exit.
tips: Bend the knees slightly if hamstrings are tight.
Push the floor away to protect the shoulders.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take deep breaths into the ribs and slowly exhale through the nose.
commonMistakes: Rounding the upper back and collapsing into the wrists.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Bhujangasana
exercises.description: Cobra pose opens the chest and helps counter long hours of desk sitting.
steps: Lie on your stomach with palms beside the lower ribs.
Press lightly into the hands and lift the chest while keeping the pelvis grounded.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the chest down slowly and rest the forehead.
tips: Lift only as high as the lower back feels comfortable.
Keep elbows close to the body.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as the chest lifts, exhale as you lower with control.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Dhanurasana
exercises.description: Bow pose opens the front body and strengthens the entire posterior chain.
steps: Lie on your stomach and bend the knees to reach for the ankles.
Kick the feet into the hands to lift chest and thighs off the floor.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Release gently back to the mat and rest.
tips: Keep the knees about hip-width apart.
Think of lengthening forward before lifting higher.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lift the chest, exhale softly while holding.
commonMistakes: Flaring the knees too wide apart.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Naukasana
exercises.description: Boat pose challenges the core and improves balance and concentration.
steps: Sit tall with knees bent and feet on the floor.
Lean back, lift the feet, and extend the arms forward while keeping the spine long.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the feet with control and sit upright.
tips: Keep the chest proud instead of rounding.
Use bent knees until you can hold steady balance.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take slow nasal breaths while the core stays firm.
commonMistakes: Locking the breath and shaking through the pose.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Setu Bandhasana
exercises.description: Bridge pose strengthens the backside and opens the chest after prolonged sitting.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet hip-width apart.
Press through the feet to lift the hips while keeping knees tracking forward.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the spine down one segment at a time.
tips: Keep the chin slightly tucked and neck relaxed.
Squeeze glutes gently instead of over-clenching.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale at the bottom, exhale as hips rise, then breathe softly in the hold.
commonMistakes: Pushing the knees outward or inward unevenly.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Balasana
exercises.description: Child's pose gently relaxes the spine, hips, and nervous system.
steps: Kneel on the floor and sit the hips back toward the heels.
Fold the torso forward with arms extended or resting by your sides.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Walk the hands back and rise slowly when finished.
tips: Place a cushion under the hips if heels feel far away.
Let the belly soften with each exhale.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale into the back ribs, exhale and melt the chest toward the floor.
commonMistakes: Forcing the forehead down when the neck feels strained.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Vajrasana
exercises.description: Diamond pose supports digestion and trains a calm upright seated posture.
steps: Kneel and sit back on your heels with knees together or slightly apart.
Lengthen the spine and rest the hands on the thighs.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lean forward onto the hands and come out slowly if ankles feel tired.
tips: Use a folded towel between calves and thighs for comfort.
Keep the shoulders relaxed over the hips.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe quietly through the nose with a tall spine.
commonMistakes: Collapsing the chest or leaning too far back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Paschimottanasana
exercises.description: Seated forward bend stretches the back body and can help wind down the mind.
steps: Sit with legs extended and spine upright on the floor.
Hinge forward from the hips and hold the legs, ankles, or feet without forcing.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lengthen up before releasing the fold and sitting tall again.
tips: Bend the knees slightly if hamstrings pull strongly.
Lead with the chest instead of rounding first.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lengthen the spine, exhale to soften deeper into the fold.
commonMistakes: Yanking on the feet and straining the neck.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Ustrasana
exercises.description: Camel pose stretches the front body deeply and opens tight chest muscles.
steps: Kneel upright with thighs vertical and hands at the low back.
Lift the chest and gently arch back, reaching hands toward the heels if comfortable.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Support the low back and rise back up slowly.
tips: Press hips forward so the bend spreads through the whole spine.
Keep the neck long and only drop the head if it feels safe.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lift the chest, exhale to ease into the backbend.
commonMistakes: Dumping all the bend into the lower back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Halasana
exercises.description: Plow pose stretches the spine and shoulders and requires controlled entry and exit.
steps: Lie on your back and lift the legs overhead with support from the hands if needed.
Bring the feet toward the floor behind you while keeping the neck still.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Roll down very slowly through the spine to exit safely.
tips: Use a folded blanket under the shoulders if available for neck comfort.
Only go as far as you can without pain or strain.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Keep the breath slow and smooth throughout the pose.
commonMistakes: Turning the head while in the pose.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Matsyasana
exercises.description: Fish pose opens the chest and front shoulders after long hours of slouching.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and hands under the hips.
Press the elbows down to lift the chest and gently tip the crown backward.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower the chest and head back down slowly.
tips: Keep weight mostly on the elbows, not the head.
Open through the sternum instead of cranking the neck.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale into the chest, exhale slowly without collapsing.
commonMistakes: Dumping too much pressure into the neck.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Marjaryasana-Bitilasana
exercises.description: Gentle spinal flow that improves mobility and reduces desk stiffness.
steps: Come onto hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
Alternate arching the spine and rounding it one segment at a time.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Return to a neutral tabletop when the timer ends.
tips: Move slowly so each part of the spine joins the motion.
Keep pressure even through both hands and knees.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale into cow pose, exhale into cat pose.
commonMistakes: Whipping the head and tailbone too fast.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Shavasana
exercises.description: Final relaxation pose that helps the body absorb the session and calm the mind.
steps: Lie on your back with legs relaxed apart and palms facing upward.
Close the eyes and let the whole body soften into the floor.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Deepen the breath slightly, then roll to one side before sitting up.
tips: Cover the body lightly if the room feels cool.
Let the tongue and jaw relax completely.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe naturally and allow each exhale to feel longer than the inhale.
commonMistakes: Fidgeting instead of settling into stillness.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Padmasana
exercises.description: Lotus pose is a meditative seat for breathwork when the hips allow it comfortably.
steps: Sit on the floor and cross the legs into a comfortable meditative seat.
Place each foot onto the opposite thigh only if the knees and hips feel safe.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Uncross the legs gently and change sides or use a simpler seat next time.
tips: Choose easy cross-legged if full lotus is not available.
Sit on a cushion to help the knees drop lower than the hips.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Keep the breath smooth and unforced through the nose.
commonMistakes: Forcing the feet into place and straining the knees.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Kapalbhati
exercises.description: Cleansing breath practice with active exhalations that energize the body and mind.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Take a light inhale, then perform short sharp exhalations by snapping the lower belly inward.
Let the inhalations happen passively between each active exhale.
Slow the breath down, breathe normally, and rest before standing up.
tips: Keep the chest quiet and let the belly do the work.
Start slowly if you are new to forceful breathing.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Rapid active exhales with passive inhales through the nose.
commonMistakes: Puffing the cheeks or forcing giant breaths.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Anulom Vilom
exercises.description: Alternate nostril breathing that promotes calm focus and balance.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Use the right hand to close one nostril and inhale through the open side, then switch for the exhale.
Continue alternating sides with slow even inhales and exhales.
Lower the hand, breathe normally, and notice the calmer state.
tips: Keep the pressure on the nostril light.
Sit tall so the lungs have room to expand.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Inhale through one nostril, exhale through the other in a smooth steady pattern.
commonMistakes: Breathing too quickly and losing the rhythm.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Bhramari
exercises.description: Bee breath uses a humming exhale to calm stress and mental agitation.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Inhale deeply through the nose and gently close the lips.
Exhale with a smooth humming sound that vibrates the face and skull.
Pause in silence after the final hum and return to normal breathing.
tips: Keep the hum soft and soothing rather than loud.
Lengthen the exhale more than the inhale for a calming effect.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Inhale through the nose, exhale with a gentle humming sound.
commonMistakes: Squeezing the throat or pushing the sound aggressively.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Ujjayi
exercises.description: Ocean breath creates a soft throat whisper that builds focus during yoga or recovery.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Inhale through the nose while slightly narrowing the throat as if fogging a mirror with the mouth closed.
Exhale through the nose with the same gentle throat control to create a soft ocean sound.
Release the throat effort and return to natural breathing.
tips: Make the sound quiet enough that only you can hear it.
Keep the jaw and facial muscles relaxed.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Use slow nasal inhales and exhales with a soft throat whisper.
commonMistakes: Forcing a harsh raspy sound from the throat.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Sheetali
exercises.description: Cooling breath practice that can feel refreshing after heat or stress.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Curl the tongue into a tube if possible and inhale through the mouth, or sip through the teeth if not.
Close the mouth and exhale slowly through the nose.
Repeat calmly, then breathe normally for a few cycles.
tips: Practice in a clean indoor environment rather than dusty air.
Keep the inhale smooth instead of gasping.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Inhale through the curled tongue or teeth, exhale through the nose.
commonMistakes: Lifting the shoulders during the inhale.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Deep Belly Breathing
exercises.description: Diaphragmatic breathing encourages fuller breaths and a calmer nervous system.
steps: Sit upright in a quiet place with shoulders relaxed.
Place one hand on the chest and one hand on the belly.
Inhale slowly so the belly rises first, then exhale and feel it fall back down.
Continue until the breath feels steady and relaxed.
tips: Keep the chest as quiet as possible while the belly expands.
Lie down if sitting upright feels tense.
Practice on an empty or lightly filled stomach.
exercises.breathing: Inhale through the nose into the belly, exhale slowly through nose or pursed lips.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders instead of filling the belly.
Tensing the shoulders or face.
Forcing the breath more than feels comfortable.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Simple full-body cardio move that quickly increases heart rate at home.
steps: Stand tall with feet together and arms by your sides.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump feet apart as arms sweep overhead, then jump back to the start.
Land softly each rep and keep the pace rhythmic.
tips: Stay light on the balls of the feet.
Use step jacks if impact bothers your joints.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe out as arms rise, inhale as they lower.
commonMistakes: Landing stiff-legged and pounding the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.description: Fast running-in-place drill that boosts calorie burn and core engagement.
steps: Stand tall with elbows bent by your sides.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Run in place and drive the knees up toward hip height one at a time.
Pump the arms and finish with quick light contacts on the floor.
tips: Stay tall rather than leaning backward.
Think quick feet, not heavy stomps.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Use sharp exhales every few steps and keep inhaling through the nose when possible.
commonMistakes: Letting the feet slam loudly onto the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Butt Kicks
exercises.description: Low-complexity cardio drill that wakes up hamstrings and raises heart rate.
steps: Stand tall with a light bend in the elbows.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jog in place and kick heels up toward the glutes in quick alternation.
Keep the steps light and continuous until the count is complete.
tips: Keep the knees pointing down rather than forward.
Use the arms naturally to help rhythm.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe steadily and keep a smooth pace.
commonMistakes: Leaning too far forward from the hips.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Jump Squats
exercises.description: Explosive squat variation that spikes heart rate and strengthens legs.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and chest proud.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Sit into a squat, drive through the feet, and jump upward powerfully.
Land softly back into the next squat with knees bent.
tips: Keep the knees tracking over the toes.
Use a regular squat if impact feels too high.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the way down, exhale as you jump up.
commonMistakes: Landing with straight stiff knees.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Star Jumps
exercises.description: Big explosive jump that challenges coordination and full-body power.
steps: Start in a quarter squat with arms by your sides.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump up and open arms and legs wide into a star shape in the air.
Land softly, gather back to the start, and repeat.
tips: Absorb the landing quietly through bent knees.
Use smaller range if you live in an apartment.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale during the jump, inhale on the landing.
commonMistakes: Losing balance by looking down.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Skaters
exercises.description: Side-to-side power move that trains balance, legs, and fat-burning cardio.
steps: Stand on one leg with knees soft and chest slightly forward.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Leap sideways to the other leg and sweep the free leg behind you.
Keep bouncing side to side in a smooth athletic rhythm.
tips: Land over the middle of the foot, not on the toes alone.
Start with step-outs if lateral jumps are too hard.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe steadily and exhale on each push across.
commonMistakes: Letting the front knee cave inward on landing.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Tuck Jumps
exercises.description: High-impact plyometric move that drives conditioning and explosive power.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width and arms ready to swing.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump upward and lift the knees toward the chest before landing softly.
Reset your balance after each jump and repeat with control.
tips: Focus on a quiet landing more than maximum height.
Rest briefly between reps if power drops off fast.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on takeoff, inhale as you land and reset.
commonMistakes: Trying to jump again before regaining balance.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Squat Thrusts
exercises.description: Burpee-style conditioning move without the final jump, good for tight spaces.
steps: Stand tall, squat down, and place your hands on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Kick the feet back to plank, return them under the body, and stand tall.
Repeat with quick but tidy transitions.
tips: Step the feet back one at a time if needed.
Keep the core tight as the legs shoot back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as feet come back in, inhale as you move toward the floor.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips sag in the plank position.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Plank Jacks
exercises.description: Cardio-focused plank jack for apartment-friendly conditioning.
steps: Start in a strong high plank with feet together.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump or step the feet out and back in while keeping the trunk firm.
Stay light on the floor and finish each rep under control.
tips: Widen the feet a little more if balance is shaky.
Step instead of jump to reduce noise and impact.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale when feet move apart, inhale when they come together.
commonMistakes: Shoulders drifting behind the wrists.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.description: Classic HIIT staple for total-body effort and quick calorie burn.
steps: From standing, squat down and place your hands on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Jump or step back to plank, return forward, and explode up to standing.
Land softly and keep moving with good form.
tips: Use a no-push-up version to keep pace steady.
Keep the neck neutral as you lower down.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you rise up, inhale during the down phase.
commonMistakes: Collapsing the chest in the plank position.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Fast Feet
exercises.description: Rapid tiny steps in place that create a strong cardio effect without much space.
steps: Stand in a light athletic stance with knees softly bent.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Take very quick short steps in place while pumping the arms lightly.
Stay low and springy until the full count is done.
tips: Keep the heels light and contacts quiet.
Stay on a comfortable pace you can maintain cleanly.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Use short quick breaths and keep the ribcage relaxed.
commonMistakes: Standing too upright and stomping heavily.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Lateral Shuffles
exercises.description: Sideways quick-step drill that works legs and gets the heart pumping.
steps: Stand with knees bent and chest lifted in an athletic stance.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Shuffle two to three steps to one side, then reverse to the other side.
Stay low and light as you change direction.
tips: Lead with the foot in the direction you are traveling.
Take smaller shuffles if space is limited.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe naturally and exhale as you push into each direction change.
commonMistakes: Crossing the feet and losing balance.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.description: Bodyweight upper-body staple that builds pressing strength and trunk stability.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Bend the elbows to lower the chest close to the floor, then press back up.
Lock in the plank again before starting the next rep.
tips: Keep the elbows about forty-five degrees from the body.
Squeeze glutes and abs to avoid a sagging back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the way down, exhale as you press up.
commonMistakes: Flaring the elbows straight out.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Wide Push-Ups
exercises.description: Push-up variation that emphasizes the chest more strongly.
steps: Set up in a plank with hands placed wider than shoulder width.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower the chest under control, then press the floor away to return up.
Rebuild the straight body line before each next rep.
tips: Turn the hands only slightly outward if wrists need comfort.
Use knee support if the wide position feels too heavy.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale down, exhale up.
commonMistakes: Dropping the head and leading with the chin.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Ups
exercises.description: Close-hand push-up that strongly targets the triceps.
steps: Place the hands close together beneath the chest to form a diamond or narrow shape.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower the body with elbows tracking back, then press back to plank.
Pause briefly at the top to regain full-body tension.
tips: Start from knees if the close grip is too demanding.
Keep the wrists comfortable by spreading the fingers wide.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale down, exhale on the press upward.
commonMistakes: Letting the elbows flare wide away from the body.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Ups
exercises.description: Shoulder-focused press that mimics overhead pushing with bodyweight.
steps: Begin in a pike position with hips high and hands on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Bend the elbows to lower the head toward the space between the hands, then press back up.
Return to the strong pike shape between reps.
tips: Shorten the range if shoulders feel pinchy.
Keep more weight in the hands than the feet.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the descent, exhale as you press away.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders into the ears.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Knee Push-Ups
exercises.description: Regression for building push-up strength with good full-range control.
steps: Set up in a straight line from knees to head with hands under shoulders.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower the chest toward the floor, then press back up without bending at the waist.
Reset the body line at the top before the next rep.
tips: Keep hips slightly forward of the knees.
Use a folded mat under the knees for comfort.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you push up.
commonMistakes: Breaking at the hips instead of moving as one unit.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
exercises.description: Chair-based dip for the triceps and front shoulders.
steps: Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair and place hands beside the hips.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Slide the hips forward, bend the elbows to lower, and press back up.
Step the feet in and sit down carefully after the set.
tips: Keep the chair stable against a wall if needed.
Only lower until the shoulders feel comfortable.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the dip, exhale on the press up.
commonMistakes: Dropping too low and stressing the shoulders.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Superman Hold
exercises.description: Posterior-chain hold that fights rounded desk posture by training the back body.
steps: Lie on your stomach with arms extended forward and legs long.
Lift arms, chest, and legs slightly off the floor while keeping the neck neutral.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower everything down gently and rest the forehead.
tips: Think long rather than just high.
Keep the glutes lightly active to support the low back.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe softly into the belly while holding.
commonMistakes: Cranking the head upward and compressing the neck.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Arm Circles
exercises.description: Simple shoulder endurance exercise useful for warm-ups and desk breaks.
steps: Stand tall with arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Make small forward circles for half the count, then reverse direction.
Lower the arms, shake them out, and repeat if needed.
tips: Keep the shoulders down away from the ears.
Use small fast circles rather than big sloppy ones.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe naturally and keep the chest open.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders as the arms tire.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Inchworms
exercises.description: Full-body movement that warms the shoulders, core, and hamstrings.
steps: Stand tall, hinge forward, and place your hands on the floor.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Walk the hands out to plank, pause, then walk them back toward the feet.
Stand up tall before beginning the next rep.
tips: Bend the knees enough to place the hands down cleanly.
Keep the core braced during the plank phase.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale while walking out, inhale as you return and stand.
commonMistakes: Sagging the lower back in the plank.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Shoulder Taps
exercises.description: Plank drill that improves shoulder stability and anti-rotation strength.
steps: Take a high plank with feet a bit wider than hip-width.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Tap one shoulder with the opposite hand while keeping the torso steady, then switch.
Set the hand down gently every rep and stay square.
tips: Press the floor away with the supporting arm.
Move slower if the hips sway too much.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale during the tap, inhale as the hand returns.
commonMistakes: Rocking the body side to side.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Reverse Snow Angels
exercises.description: Prone shoulder exercise that strengthens upper back muscles for better posture.
steps: Lie face down with arms extended overhead and palms facing down.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift the arms slightly and sweep them out and down toward the hips, then return overhead.
Move slowly and keep the forehead relaxed toward the floor.
tips: Keep thumbs pointing upward if shoulders feel better that way.
Use a small lift rather than over-arching the back.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the arms sweep down, inhale as they return.
commonMistakes: Jerking the arms instead of using the upper back muscles.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.description: Beginner-friendly push-up performed against a wall for low joint stress.
steps: Stand facing a wall with hands placed at chest height.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Bend the elbows to bring the chest toward the wall, then press back out.
Keep the body straight from head to heels throughout.
tips: Walk the feet farther back to make it harder.
Keep wrists in line with the forearms.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lean in, exhale as you press away.
commonMistakes: Bending at the hips and breaking the body line.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squats
exercises.description: Fundamental leg exercise for strength, mobility, and calorie burn.
steps: Stand with feet about shoulder-width and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Sit the hips back and down, then drive through the feet to stand tall.
Finish each rep by squeezing the glutes without leaning back.
tips: Keep the chest proud and knees tracking over toes.
Use a chair behind you as a depth guide if needed.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale on the descent, exhale as you stand.
commonMistakes: Letting the heels lift off the floor.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Sumo Squats
exercises.description: Wide-stance squat that targets inner thighs and glutes.
steps: Take a wide stance with toes turned out more than a regular squat.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower straight down between the hips and press back up through the whole foot.
Stand tall and reset your posture between reps.
tips: Keep the knees moving in line with the toes.
Do not force extra depth if hips feel pinchy.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you rise.
commonMistakes: Folding forward and losing the upright chest.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Lunges
exercises.description: Forward lunge builds single-leg strength, balance, and leg definition.
steps: Stand tall with feet under hips and hands on hips or chest.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Step one foot forward, bend both knees, and push back to the start before switching sides.
Keep the torso tall and land softly each rep.
tips: Take a step long enough so the front knee stays above the ankle.
Lightly tap the back knee toward the floor instead of crashing it.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you push back.
commonMistakes: Letting the front knee collapse inward.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunges
exercises.description: Backward lunge that is often friendlier on the knees than forward lunges.
steps: Stand tall and step one foot back behind you.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower into a lunge, then drive through the front foot to return and switch sides.
Stay balanced and controlled rather than rushing the step.
tips: Keep most of the weight in the front heel.
Use a small step back if balance is limited.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you stand up.
commonMistakes: Pushing off mainly from the back foot.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Jump Squats
exercises.description: Explosive lower-body move for power and a stronger calorie burn.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width and sink into a squat.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Drive upward into a jump and land back into the next squat softly.
Pause if needed to keep every landing clean and quiet.
tips: Land with knees bent and chest lifted.
Use bodyweight squats if jumping irritates joints.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale down, exhale on takeoff.
commonMistakes: Landing with knees caving inward.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.description: Isometric leg hold that quickly builds thigh endurance without equipment.
steps: Stand with your back against a wall and feet a step forward.
Slide down until knees and hips are roughly bent to ninety degrees.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Press through the feet to stand up when the timer ends.
tips: Keep the knees stacked above the ankles.
Place hands lightly on thighs only if balance is needed.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take slow steady breaths while keeping the abs lightly engaged.
commonMistakes: Sliding too low and straining the knees.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Glute Bridges
exercises.description: Hip bridge that strengthens glutes and supports lower-back comfort.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat near the hips.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Press through the heels to lift the hips until the body forms a line from knees to shoulders.
Lower one vertebra at a time and repeat.
tips: Keep the ribs down so the movement comes from the hips.
Drive through the heels more than the toes.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the hips rise, inhale as you lower.
commonMistakes: Overarching the lower back at the top.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Single Leg Bridges
exercises.description: Unilateral bridge that builds glute strength and pelvic stability.
steps: Set up for a bridge and extend one leg straight without moving the thighs.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Press through the grounded heel to lift the hips, then lower with control before switching sides.
Keep the pelvis level throughout each rep.
tips: Start with a smaller lift if the hamstring cramps.
Keep the working foot close enough to the hips.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale up, inhale down.
commonMistakes: Twisting the hips as you lift.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.description: Simple lower-leg exercise that improves ankle strength and walking stamina.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width and fingertips on a wall for balance if needed.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Rise up onto the balls of your feet, pause briefly, and lower slowly.
Finish the set standing tall with the heels grounded.
tips: Move through the full range instead of bouncing.
Keep weight even across the big toe and little toe.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you rise, inhale as you lower.
commonMistakes: Dropping the heels quickly without control.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Donkey Kicks
exercises.description: Mat move for glute activation and hip control.
steps: Come to hands and knees with the spine neutral.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Kick one bent leg upward by pressing the heel toward the ceiling, then lower and switch sides.
Keep the core steady so the low back does not sway.
tips: Lift only as high as the glute can control.
Square the hips to the floor throughout.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the heel drives up, inhale as the knee lowers.
commonMistakes: Arching the back to fake extra height.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Fire Hydrants
exercises.description: Outer-hip exercise that helps strengthen glute medius and hip stability.
steps: Start on hands and knees with a neutral spine.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift one knee out to the side without shifting the torso, then lower and switch.
Move slowly so the working hip does the lifting.
tips: Keep the arms straight but not locked.
Think of shining the knee to the side, not rolling open the whole body.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as the knee lifts, inhale as it lowers.
commonMistakes: Leaning heavily into the opposite shoulder.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Side Leg Raises
exercises.description: Simple floor exercise for outer thighs, hip stability, and glute tone.
steps: Lie on one side with legs long and head supported comfortably.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift the top leg to hip height or slightly higher, then lower with control before switching sides.
Keep the toes facing forward instead of up toward the ceiling.
tips: Stay long through the waist rather than collapsing into the floor.
Use a small range if the hip pinches.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the lift, inhale on the lowering phase.
commonMistakes: Rolling the top hip backward.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.description: Core endurance hold for full trunk stability and better posture.
steps: Set forearms under shoulders and extend the legs behind you.
Lift the body into one straight line and brace the core firmly.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower to the floor with control when time is up.
tips: Keep the head in line with the spine.
Squeeze the glutes to protect the lower back.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Use calm nasal breaths while bracing the abs.
commonMistakes: Piking the hips too high.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: Core stability hold for the obliques and lateral chain.
steps: Stack the feet and place one elbow under the shoulder.
Lift the hips so the body forms a straight line from ankles to head.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower slowly and switch to the other side.
tips: Use a bent-bottom-knee option if full version is too hard.
Press the floor away and keep the neck relaxed.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take smooth ribcage breaths while keeping the waist lifted.
commonMistakes: Letting the top shoulder roll forward.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.description: Foundational anti-extension exercise for safer, stronger core control.
steps: Lie on your back with knees over hips and arms pointing upward.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Reach opposite arm and leg away while keeping the ribs tucked down.
Return to start and alternate sides slowly.
tips: Move with control more than speed.
Press the lower back into the mat each rep.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the reach, inhale on the return.
commonMistakes: Letting the belly pop up and ribs flare.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.description: Cross-body stability drill that strengthens the back line and improves balance.
steps: Start on hands and knees with the spine neutral.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Reach one arm forward and the opposite leg back without shifting your torso.
Bring them back in and switch sides slowly.
tips: Think of balancing a glass of water on your low back.
Reach long through fingers and heel instead of lifting too high.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you reach, inhale as you return.
commonMistakes: Twisting the hips or shoulders open.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Hollow Hold
exercises.description: Gymnast-style hold that builds deep anterior core strength.
steps: Lie on your back and press the lower back firmly into the floor.
Lift shoulders and legs slightly off the ground while keeping ribs tucked.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Bring the knees in or lower down once the timer ends.
tips: Bend the knees or keep arms by sides to scale it down.
Quality matters more than making the hold look dramatic.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take shallow but steady breaths without losing the back position.
commonMistakes: Arching the back to keep legs too low.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Superman
exercises.description: Back-body hold that balances heavy front-core work and supports posture.
steps: Lie face down with arms extended and legs long.
Lift arms and legs slightly while keeping the neck in line.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Lower smoothly and rest the forehead when time is complete.
tips: Keep the glutes lightly on instead of squeezing too hard.
Lengthen away through fingers and toes.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe quietly into the abdomen while holding.
commonMistakes: Jerking upward and compressing the low back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Plank Walk
exercises.description: Dynamic plank drill that trains the core to resist rotation during movement.
steps: Begin in a forearm plank or high plank with a tight midline.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Move one hand or forearm at a time to travel between positions or slightly side to side.
Keep the body steady and controlled for every step.
tips: Take smaller steps to minimize hip sway.
Widen the feet if balance is challenging.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on each step, inhale between changes.
commonMistakes: Rushing and bouncing through the shoulders.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Ab Roll-out
exercises.description: Towel rollout on a smooth floor that challenges anti-extension core strength.
steps: Kneel on the floor with hands on a folded towel placed in front of you.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Slide the towel forward as the hips stay tucked, then pull back using the abs.
Return to tall kneeling and reset before the next rep.
tips: Only roll out as far as you can keep the ribs down.
Use extra padding under the knees for comfort.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale as you pull back in, inhale as you glide forward.
commonMistakes: Letting the lower back arch during the rollout.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Neck Rolls
exercises.description: Gentle neck mobility drill to reduce stiffness from long screen time.
steps: Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Slowly roll the head in a comfortable semicircle or full circle, then reverse direction.
Return to a neutral head position and pause.
tips: Move only through a pain-free range.
Keep the shoulders soft and heavy.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and avoid holding tension in the jaw.
commonMistakes: Rolling too fast and compressing the neck.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Shoulder Stretch
exercises.description: Cross-body shoulder stretch for easing tight delts and upper back tension.
steps: Stand tall and bring one arm across the chest.
Use the other arm to hug it closer without lifting the shoulder.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Release gently and switch arms.
tips: Keep the shoulder of the stretching arm relaxed down.
Stand tall instead of twisting the torso.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take slow calm breaths and soften deeper on each exhale.
commonMistakes: Yanking the arm aggressively across the body.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Chest Opener
exercises.description: Simple chest stretch that counters rounded shoulders from desk posture.
steps: Stand or kneel tall and clasp the hands behind your back if comfortable.
Lift the chest and gently draw the knuckles away from the body.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Release the hands slowly and shake out the arms.
tips: Bend the elbows if clasping straight arms feels intense.
Keep the chin level rather than jutting it forward.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to widen the chest, exhale and soften the shoulders down.
commonMistakes: Forcing the shoulders back with pain.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Quad Stretch
exercises.description: Standing thigh stretch that helps after squats, walking, or sitting.
steps: Stand tall and hold a wall for balance if needed.
Bend one knee and draw the heel toward the glute while keeping knees close together.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Release slowly and switch legs.
tips: Tuck the tailbone slightly to deepen the stretch safely.
Keep the chest upright instead of leaning forward.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and relax the front of the thigh on each exhale.
commonMistakes: Pulling the foot so hard the knee feels strained.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description: Standing hamstring stretch for the back of the legs and lower back comfort.
steps: Stand with one heel slightly forward and toes pulled upward.
Hinge at the hips with a long spine until you feel the stretch behind the thigh.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Stand back up slowly and switch legs.
tips: Keep a soft bend in the standing knee.
Reach the hips back instead of rounding the spine.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to lengthen, exhale to hinge a little deeper.
commonMistakes: Locking the knee and forcing the stretch.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Lunge-based stretch for tight front hips caused by prolonged sitting.
steps: Step one foot forward into a split stance or low kneeling lunge.
Tuck the pelvis slightly and shift forward until the front of the rear hip stretches.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Ease out gently and switch sides.
tips: Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Use a smaller stance if balance is difficult.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale tall, exhale and gently sink into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Overarching the low back instead of tucking the pelvis.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow Stretch
exercises.description: Gentle mobility stretch for the entire spine and ribcage.
steps: Come onto hands and knees with a neutral tabletop position.
Alternate between arching and rounding the spine in a slow flowing pattern.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Return to neutral when the time ends and sit back to rest.
tips: Move with your breath rather than with speed.
Keep the hands pressing evenly into the floor.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale into cow, exhale into cat.
commonMistakes: Throwing the head back sharply.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Spinal Twist
exercises.description: Lying spinal twist helps release the lower back and outer hips.
steps: Lie on your back and draw one knee toward the chest.
Guide the knee across the body while the opposite shoulder stays heavy on the floor.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Come back through center and repeat on the other side.
tips: Use a cushion under the knee if the twist feels too intense.
Keep the twist easy and restorative rather than forced.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Take slow breaths into the belly and soften more on each exhale.
commonMistakes: Pulling the knee down aggressively and straining the back.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Desk Neck Rolls
exercises.description: Quick desk break move to release neck stiffness during long workdays.
steps: Sit tall near the front of your chair with feet flat.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Roll the head gently from shoulder to shoulder and complete easy circles, then reverse.
Pause upright and let the neck settle before returning to work.
tips: Keep the circles slow and comfortable.
Drop the shoulders away from the ears.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe slowly and unclench the jaw.
commonMistakes: Moving too quickly and making yourself dizzy.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Seated Shoulder Shrugs
exercises.description: Mini posture reset that relaxes the upper traps after screen work.
steps: Sit or stand tall with arms relaxed by your sides.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift the shoulders toward the ears, pause briefly, then release them down slowly.
Repeat in a relaxed rhythm without tensing the neck.
tips: Make the lowering phase slower than the lifting phase.
Keep the chin level and chest open.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as shoulders rise, exhale as they melt down.
commonMistakes: Jerking the shoulders instead of moving smoothly.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Seated Spinal Twist
exercises.description: Chair-friendly twist that refreshes the mid-back after meetings and calls.
steps: Sit tall on a chair with both feet flat on the floor.
Turn the torso gently to one side using the chair back for light support.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Return to center slowly and repeat on the other side.
tips: Lengthen the spine before turning deeper.
Keep the twist comfortable and smooth.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale to sit taller, exhale to rotate gently.
commonMistakes: Forcing the neck farther than the torso turns.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.

exercises.name: Wrist Circles
exercises.description: Small mobility drill to ease typing and mouse-related wrist stiffness.
steps: Extend your arms forward or rest elbows by your sides.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Make smooth circles through the wrists in one direction, then reverse.
Shake the hands out and finish with relaxed fingers.
tips: Spread the fingers wide to encourage mobility.
Keep the circles slow and controlled.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Breathe naturally and relax the forearms.
commonMistakes: Moving only the fingers instead of the wrists.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Desk Chair Squats
exercises.description: Quick office-friendly leg wake-up using your chair as a depth guide.
steps: Stand in front of a stable chair with feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Sit the hips back to lightly tap the chair, then stand back up strongly.
Repeat smoothly without dropping your full weight heavily onto the seat.
tips: Keep the chest lifted and weight through the heels.
Use the chair only as a target, not for support if possible.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you sit back, exhale as you stand.
commonMistakes: Letting the knees shoot far forward and heels lift.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raises
exercises.description: Desk break move to promote circulation after sitting for hours.
steps: Stand tall near a desk or wall for balance.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lift the heels up as high as comfortable, pause, and lower them slowly.
Keep the body upright and finish grounded through the full foot.
tips: Use the desk lightly rather than leaning on it.
Pause at the top for a stronger calf squeeze.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Exhale on the lift, inhale on the descent.
commonMistakes: Bouncing quickly instead of controlling the movement.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Desk Push-Ups
exercises.description: Incline push-up against a desk for a fast upper-body energizer.
steps: Place hands on a sturdy desk and walk feet back into a straight body line.
Brace your core and keep your posture steady.
Lower the chest toward the desk edge, then press back out strongly.
Stay rigid through the torso for every rep.
tips: Check that the desk is stable before starting.
Keep elbows angled slightly back rather than straight out.
Stop the set when your form starts to slip.
exercises.breathing: Inhale as you lower, exhale as you press away.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips sag toward the desk.
Rushing through the movement.
Holding your breath instead of using a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Standing lunge stretch to undo tight hips from back-to-back sitting.
steps: Step one foot back into a gentle split stance while holding a chair or desk if needed.
Tuck the pelvis slightly and bend the front knee until the rear hip feels a stretch.
Hold the position for the target time with calm, even control.
Come back to standing and switch sides slowly.
tips: Keep the torso tall instead of arching the back.
Use a shorter stance if balance feels wobbly.
Keep your face and jaw relaxed while you work.
exercises.breathing: Inhale tall, exhale and melt into the front of the rear hip.
commonMistakes: Leaning far forward and missing the hip stretch.
Letting posture collapse before the timer ends.
Forgetting to keep the core gently engaged.
